frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Arre, career career bolne se ghar thodi chalta hai. Shaadi ke baad aurat ki izzat uske ghar sambhalne aur bachche paida karne se hi banti hai, Islam mein bhi biwi ko apni zimmedari samajhni chahiye. Mujhe lagta hai Sana ka shohar usko theek se samjha nahi raha, mard agar raasta na dikhaye toh aurat duniya ke pichhe bhaagti rehti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment is misogynistic because it defines a woman's respect and role primarily through motherhood, domesticity, and obedience within marriage. It also frames women as needing male guidance and judges the husband for not controlling or directing his wife properly.
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 48. Married. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 26 year old daughter Sana has told you and your wife that she and her husband have decided to wait at least three more years before having children because they both want to establish their careers first. You are sitting with your wife after Sana has left. You are telling your wife what you think of this decision and what it means about the kind of wife Sana is and what Islam says about a woman's role in marriage and why you think Sana's husband is not guiding her correctly.
